issues.description: Unused private field 'data' in MockDatabase class
issues.suggestion: Remove the unused 'data' field or implement it properly if mock database functionality is needed

issues.description: Use of 'any' type in Database interface params reduces type safety
issues.suggestion: Replace 'any[]' with a more specific type, such as 'unknown[]' or a generic type parameter

issues.description: Mock database query always returns empty array, making it non-functional for testing
issues.suggestion: Implement a proper mock with in-memory storage or use a mocking library like jest.mock()

issues.description: Parameters 'sql' and 'params' are declared but never used in the mock implementation
issues.suggestion: Either implement proper mock logic using these parameters or add eslint-disable comment if intentionally unused

codeQuality.summary: The database module provides a simple interface but has a non-functional mock implementation. The unused private field and lack of proper mock functionality significantly impact maintainability and testability. Type safety could be improved by avoiding 'any' types.

untestedPaths.reasoning: The MockDatabase is exported and used throughout the application but has no tests verifying its behavior
This is the core database query method used by all CRUD operations, but it always returns empty arrays which could cause bugs
No tests verify that the mock properly implements the Database interface contract

testCoverage.summary: The database module has zero test coverage. The MockDatabase class is completely untested, which is critical since it's used throughout the application. The mock's non-functional implementation (always returning empty arrays) means integration tests will also fail.

suggestions.description: Replace non-functional MockDatabase with a proper in-memory implementation or use a testing library
suggestions.benefits: Provides functional mock database for testing, eliminates bugs caused by always-empty results, makes integration tests possible

suggestions.description: Replace 'any' type with generic type parameters for better type safety
suggestions.benefits: Improves type safety by allowing callers to specify expected return types, reduces runtime errors from type mismatches

suggestions.description: Export MockDatabase class to allow for proper dependency injection and testing
suggestions.benefits: Enables easier testing by allowing test files to create isolated database instances, supports dependency injection patterns

refactorings.summary: The database module needs significant refactoring to be functional. The mock implementation should either store data in-memory or the module should use a proper mocking library. Type safety improvements and better export patterns would enhance testability and maintainability.

issues.description: Test file only imports and tests 2 functions out of 8 exported functions, indicating incomplete test coverage
issues.suggestion: Add test suites for createTodo, getAllTodos, getTodoById, updateTodoStatus, and deleteTodo functions

issues.description: No setup/teardown for database state between tests
issues.suggestion: Add beforeEach/afterEach hooks to reset database state and prevent test interdependencies

issues.description: createTodo is imported but never tested, yet it depends on database interactions that could fail
issues.suggestion: Add integration tests that mock the database and verify createTodo behavior

issues.description: Missing edge case tests for validateTodoInput (null input, whitespace-only title, etc.)
issues.suggestion: Add tests for: null input, undefined fields, whitespace-only strings, special characters in boundaries

codeQuality.summary: Test file provides basic coverage for validation and sanitization functions but completely lacks tests for the critical CRUD operations. No database mocking or integration tests are present. The test suite needs significant expansion to properly verify application behavior.

untestedPaths.reasoning: createTodo is the core function for creating todos with database interaction, validation, and sanitization - must be tested
getAllTodos retrieves all todos from database - needs tests to verify query execution and response parsing
getTodoById handles database queries with user input (id) - must be tested for SQL injection protection and null handling
updateTodoStatus modifies database state - needs tests to verify update logic and parameter binding
deleteTodo permanently removes data - must be tested to prevent accidental data loss
Missing tests for boundary conditions and edge cases in validation logic

testCoverage.summary: Test coverage is severely lacking. Only 2 out of 8 functions are tested (validateTodoInput and sanitizeInput). All database interaction functions (createTodo, getAllTodos, getTodoById, updateTodoStatus, deleteTodo) have zero test coverage. Estimated coverage: 25% of functionality.

suggestions.description: Add comprehensive test suites for all CRUD operations with database mocking
suggestions.benefits: Provides comprehensive test coverage for all functions, uses proper mocking to isolate units, enables catching bugs before production

suggestions.description: Add test fixtures and setup/teardown hooks for consistent test state
suggestions.benefits: Improves test maintainability by centralizing test data, makes tests more readable, easier to add new test cases

suggestions.description: Extract database mock setup into reusable helper functions
suggestions.benefits: Makes database mocking consistent across tests, reduces boilerplate code, improves test readability

refactorings.summary: The test file needs major expansion to cover all CRUD operations. Add database mocking, test fixtures, and comprehensive test suites for createTodo, getAllTodos, getTodoById, updateTodoStatus, and deleteTodo. Current tests are well-structured but represent only a small fraction of needed coverage.

issues.description: Validation checks title.length before verifying title exists, could cause runtime error
issues.suggestion: Reorder checks: verify title exists first, then check length. Current line 'if (input.title.length > 200)' should come after null/undefined check

issues.description: Using Math.random() for ID generation is not cryptographically secure and vulnerable to prediction
issues.suggestion: Replace with a proper UUID library like 'uuid' or 'crypto.randomUUID()': import { v4 as uuidv4 } from 'uuid'; return uuidv4();

issues.description: ID generation using Date.now() and Math.random() is not collision-safe in high-concurrency scenarios
issues.suggestion: Use a UUID library to guarantee unique IDs: import { randomUUID } from 'crypto'; return randomUUID();

issues.description: Sanitization occurs after validation, but validation checks unsanitized length which could bypass limits
issues.suggestion: Either validate length after sanitization, or validate on raw input and document this behavior. Consider: const trimmed = input.title.trim(); if (!trimmed || trimmed.length > 200) return false;

issues.description: Type assertion 'as Todo[]' without validation - database could return malformed data
issues.suggestion: Add runtime validation using a schema validator like Zod: const TodoSchema = z.array(z.object({...})); return TodoSchema.parse(rows);

issues.description: Type assertion 'as Todo' without validating the structure of rows[0]
issues.suggestion: Validate the returned data structure before casting: if (!isValidTodo(rows[0])) throw new Error('Invalid todo data'); return rows[0];

issues.description: Empty string default for description could be confusing - explicit undefined might be clearer
issues.suggestion: Consider: const sanitizedDescription = input.description ? sanitizeInput(input.description) : undefined;

issues.description: sanitizeInput uses multiple replace calls which is inefficient for large strings
issues.suggestion: Consider using a single pass with a character map or regex: input.replace(/[<>"']/g, (char) => charMap[char])

codeQuality.summary: The todo module has good structure and uses parameterized queries to prevent SQL injection, but has several critical issues: insecure ID generation using Math.random(), lack of runtime type validation on database responses, and validation logic that could be bypassed. The code needs stronger input validation, secure ID generation, and proper response validation.

untestedPaths.reasoning: Core business logic combining validation, sanitization, and database operations - must be thoroughly tested
Retrieves all todos with ORDER BY clause - needs tests to verify query structure and response handling
Handles user input (id) in SQL query - critical for testing SQL injection protection and null handling
Modifies database state - must verify parameter binding order and update execution
Permanently deletes data - must be tested to prevent data loss bugs
ID generation must be unique and unpredictable - needs tests for format and uniqueness
Need to test exact boundary conditions (200 chars, 1000 chars) and edge cases
Need to test database error scenarios and rollback behavior

testCoverage.summary: Test coverage is critically low at approximately 20%. Only validateTodoInput and sanitizeInput have tests. All 5 CRUD operations (createTodo, getAllTodos, getTodoById, updateTodoStatus, deleteTodo) and the generateId function are completely untested. Database interaction logic, error handling, and edge cases have zero coverage.

suggestions.description: Replace Math.random() based ID generation with cryptographically secure UUID
suggestions.benefits: Guarantees unique IDs, prevents ID prediction attacks, eliminates collision risk in concurrent scenarios, follows security best practices

suggestions.location: Type assertions in getAllTodos and getTodoById (lines 81, 95)
suggestions.description: Add runtime validation for database responses using schema validation
suggestions.benefits: Catches malformed database responses at runtime, prevents type errors, provides clear error messages, improves reliability

suggestions.description: Optimize multiple replace() calls with single-pass implementation
suggestions.benefits: Improves performance by reducing string operations from 4 to 1, more maintainable escape map, easier to extend with new characters

suggestions.description: Extract todo object construction into separate function for testability
suggestions.benefits: Separates concerns, makes todo construction testable independently of database, improves code organization

suggestions.description: Enhance validation to provide specific error messages instead of boolean
suggestions.benefits: Provides specific error messages to users, enables better error handling, improves API usability, allows displaying field-specific errors in UI

refactorings.summary: The todo module needs critical refactoring: replace Math.random() ID generation with UUID, add runtime type validation for database responses, optimize sanitization function, extract todo construction logic, and enhance validation to return specific errors. These changes will significantly improve security, reliability, and maintainability.

recommendations.description: Replace Math.random() based ID generation with cryptographically secure UUID (crypto.randomUUID() or uuid library). Current implementation is vulnerable to ID prediction attacks and collision risks.

Add comprehensive tests for all CRUD operations (createTodo, getAllTodos, getTodoById, updateTodoStatus, deleteTodo). Current test coverage is only 20-25%, leaving critical database interactions untested.
Implement functional MockDatabase class or use proper mocking library. Current mock always returns empty arrays, making integration tests impossible and potentially causing bugs in production.
Add runtime validation for database responses using schema validation (e.g., Zod). Type assertions 'as Todo' and 'as Todo[]' are unsafe and could allow malformed data through.
Fix validation logic ordering bug where title.length is checked before verifying title exists. This could cause runtime errors with null/undefined input.
Add database mocking infrastructure to test files with setup/teardown hooks and test fixtures. This enables proper isolation and repeatability of tests.
Replace 'any' types with proper type annotations or generics to improve type safety throughout the codebase.
Enhance validation to return specific error messages instead of boolean, providing better user feedback and debugging information.
